description: UK online retailer transactions, Dec 2009-Dec 2011. Substituted for the gated Kaggle 'retail-scanner-data' notebook dataset (marian447), which itself appears to derive from this same UCI source (5,305 SKUs here vs. the Kaggle listing's stated 5,242). Transaction-level, real invoice/quantity/price/customer data -- suitable directly for log-log elasticity regression.
note: 5305 unique SKUs, 5942 unique customers, 43 countries. 6207 rows with Price<=0 and 22950 rows with negative Quantity (returns) -- filter both before fitting elasticity.

description: Monash Time Series Forecasting Archive's 'Dominick Dataset': weekly per-SKU profit, reformatted from Kilts Center Dominick's Finer Foods scanner data. No store ID, UPC, category label, or calendar date -- series are anonymized (T1..T115704) and 'Week_Index' is a per-series relative index, not a date. Useful for time-series elasticity modeling, not for category-level breakdowns (see cheese.csv / dominicks_combined.csv, both manual_required).
date_range: relative week 0-392 per series, no calendar dates
note: 115704 unique series. 9366843 of 19092987 rows are zero-profit weeks (no sale that week, not missing data).

description: 64,682 transactions of 5,242 SKUs from 22,625 customers over one year.

description: Small volume/price/marketing-activity dataset for prototyping. The Dominick's raw data does include a cheese category, but the Monash reformatted archive used for monash_dominicks.csv anonymizes series (T1, T2, ...) with no category label, so a cheese-only subset can't be recovered from it. Left undownloaded rather than fabricated.
note: Extract the cheese category from a registered Kilts Center Dominick's download, if/when available.
